title: Tissue Freezing in Cryostor Solution and Processing
author: Regina Hoo
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.bgsnjwde
materials: Most tissue is collected in Hyperthermosol and kept on ice or at 4 °C .
Discard supernant. Proceed with preferred tissue digestion protocol

steps.title: Place tissue in petri dish, add...
steps.instruction: Place tissue in petri dish, add enough ice cold RPMI/10% FBS to cover the tissue (depend on tissue size).

steps.title: Mince tissue with 2 scapels, just...
steps.instruction: Mince tissue with 2 scapels, just like what we would do for enzymatic digestion.

steps.title: Carefully transfer minced tissue into a 50ml falcon tube
steps.instruction: Carefully transfer minced tissue into a 50ml falcon tube.

steps.title: Wash and rinse the petri dish...
steps.instruction: Wash and rinse the petri dish with plenty of RPMI/10% FBS. Transfer the RPMI/10%FBS and remaining tissue pieces into the falcon tube.

steps.title: Resuspend the tissue in 1 ml...
steps.instruction: Resuspend the tissue in 1 ml of ice cod CS10 and transfer into a cryovial.

steps.title: Rinse the falcon tube with 0
steps.instruction: Rinse the falcon tube with 0.8 ml of extra cold CS10 and transfer into the cryovial.

steps.title: Place cryovial in CoolCell and leave...
steps.instruction: Place cryovial in CoolCell and leave in -80 °C freezer overnight.

steps.title: Remove cryovial from CoolCell next day,...
steps.instruction: Remove cryovial from CoolCell next day, and store cryovial in designated box in -80 °C .

steps.title: To process cryopreserved tissue, thaw cryovials...
steps.instruction: To process cryopreserved tissue, thaw cryovials in water bath at 37 °C .

steps.title: Transfer thawed tissue into a falcon...
steps.instruction: Transfer thawed tissue into a falcon tube containing 20ml warm RPMI/10% FBS.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 500 x g for 4 minutes
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 500 x g for 4 minutes.
